title: Equivalent Fraction vs Simplest Form
summary_text: Equivalent fractions name the same value, while simplest form asks for the most reduced name for that value.

atoms.atom_text: Represents the same value using a different numerator and denominator.
atoms.explanation_text: Equivalent fractions are different names for the same amount.

atoms.atom_text: Writes the fraction after dividing by the greatest common factor.
atoms.explanation_text: Simplest form is the most reduced version of a fraction.

atoms.atom_text: 2/4 and 1/2 belong together as an example pair.
atoms.explanation_text: Those two fractions are equivalent because they represent the same value.
Simplest form asks for the reduced version, not just another matching fraction.

atoms.atom_text: Can appear in the same worked example about 2/4 and 1/2.
atoms.explanation_text: The same example can teach both ideas, but each idea answers a different question.

atoms.atom_text: Add the numerator and denominator together to get the answer.
atoms.explanation_text: Adding the numerator and denominator does not define equivalent fractions or simplest form.
